channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Branch knocked down, 8th May
item.description:
A lorry going fast has boken a branch on a tree which is hanging down blocking access to 102 Woodside Avenue
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woodside Avenue, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garbage on pavement, 8th May
item.description:
This morning I found someone’s garbage bag ruptured with all its rubbish strewn on the pavement right in front of the gate of my house.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Wellington Avenue, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Shocking accumulation of litter, 8th May
item.description:
The whole road is covered with debris on pavements and in the gutter. There is broken glass, plastic and countless nails in the gutter. In the last month I’ve had two nails in my tyres. Not sure what we pay such high council tax for. I was told over two months ago a sweeping team would be sent and the same litter remains. It’s the whole street. I see other streets being swept and weeded. Why is ours always left out?

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked drain in Tettenhall Road, 8th May
item.description:
This drain is completely blocked. It is near No.202 Tottenhall Rd

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tottenhall Road, Palmers Green
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Builders have closed the road off on Orsman road, illegally, 8th May
item.description:
Builders have illegally closed off Orsman road, near the corner of Kingsland, to move girders into the building on the corner. This has happened every day this week. They have taped off the entrance to our building. Also, they started work at 6.50am today.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kingsland Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lamppost light not working, 8th May
item.description:
Out for weeks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Shearwood Crescent, North End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned damaged car, 8th May
item.description:
This car has been involved in an accident and has been left for a number of weeks causing a hazard for cars/vehicles passing on an extremely busy road!
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 173, Blackfen Road, Blackfen, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped clothes and rubbish, 8th May
item.description:
People are dumping on existing rubbish as the area has not been cleared

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Battersby Road, Catford South, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Issue regarding improper waste disposal, 8th May
item.description:
Resident is repeatedly leaving household rubbish outside rather than placing it securely in a bin or disposing of it correctly. This is happening frequently and without consideration for the surrounding area. As a result, the rubbish is being torn open by foxes, causing waste to be scattered across the pavement and surrounding area. On other occasions, waste is simply left exposed and not disposed of properly at all. This creates an unsightly environment and poses a potential public health risk. There is a growing concern that this behaviour may attract rodents and contribute to the spread of bacteria, which could affect nearby households if not addressed promptly. I have attached a photograph as evidence of the issue. I would be grateful if this matter could be investigated and appropriate action taken to ensure proper waste management and prevent further occurrences.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Framlingham Crescent, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multiple balloon canisters and rubbish, 8th May
item.description:
Here for three days.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Clissold Crescent, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Garbage everywhere. None of the cleaners ever clean this area and it is a mess., 8th May
item.description:
Garbage everywhere. None of the cleaners ever clean this area and it is a mess.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 16, Northwold Road, West Hackney, Greater London

item.title: This is now a public hygiene and environmental health issue, not simply a minor litter complaint., 8th May
item.description:
This location has become a serious hygiene problem and residents are extremely concerned. Every morning we step outside to scattered rubbish, food waste and overflowing litter around the public bin area. The waste is being left repeatedly and is attracting rats and other vermin directly outside residents’ homes. This issue has already been reported many times, yet previous cases keep being closed without any proper or lasting resolution. The problem continues on a daily basis.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 89, High Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
